title: Theory of Computation - Context Free Language

questions.question:
\[ S \to aSa \mid bSb\mid a\mid b \]
The language generated by the above grammar over the alphabet \( \{a,b\} \) is the set of:



Theory of Computation: GATE CSE 2009 | Question: 12, ISRO2016
options: strings that begin and end with the same symbol

questions.question:
Context free languages are closed under



Theory of Computation: ISRO CSE 2020 | Question: 37

Which f the following statements is FALSE?



Theory of Computation: ISRO CSE 2023 | Question: 56
options: The intersection of a regular language and a context-free language is context=free
The intersection of a regular language and context-free language is regular
The union of two context-free languages is context-free
The union of two regular languages is regular
questions.correct_answer: The intersection of a regular language and context-free language is regular

questions.question:
Which of the following are context-free?

\( A=\{a^n b^n\, a^mb^m\mid m,n\geq0\} \)
\( B=\{a^m b^n\, a^mb^n\mid m,n\geq0\} \)
\( C=\{a^mb^n\mid m\neq 2n,\,m,n\geq0\} \)



Theory of Computation: ISRO
